old.claim_text: The causes of World War II included unresolved tensions in the aftermath of World War I, the rise of fascism in Europe and militarism in Japan.
old.subject: The causes

new.claim_text: The causes of World War II included unresolved tensions in the aftermath of World War I, and the rise of fascism in Europe and militarism in Japan.
new.subject: The causes

old.claim_text: In 1940, the Soviet Union annexed the Baltic states and parts of Finland and Romania, while Germany conquered Norway, Belgium, Luxembourg and the Netherlands.
old.subject: the Soviet Union

qualifiers.spatial: the Soviet Union, Baltic, Finland, Romania, Germany, Norway, Belgium, Luxembourg, Netherlands

new.claim_text: In 1940, the Soviet Union annexed the Baltic states and parts of Finland and Romania, while Germany conquered Norway, Denmark, Belgium, Luxembourg, and the Netherlands.
new.subject: the Soviet Union

qualifiers.spatial: the Soviet Union, Baltic, Finland, Romania, Germany, Norway, Denmark, Belgium, Luxembourg, Netherlands

old.claim_text: After the fall of France in June 1940, the war continued mainly between Germany, now assisted by Fascist Italy, and the British Empire, with fighting in the Balkans, Mediterranean, and Middle East, East Africa, the aerial Battle of Britain and the Blitz, and the naval Battle of the Atlantic.
old.subject: the war

qualifiers.spatial: France, Germany, the British Empire, Balkans, Mediterranean, Middle East, East Africa, Battle, Britain

new.claim_text: After the fall of France in June 1940, the war continued mainly between Germany, now assisted by Fascist Italy, and the British Empire/British Commonwealth, with fighting in the Balkans, Mediterranean, and Middle East, East Africa, the aerial Battle of Britain and the Blitz, and the naval Battle of the Atlantic.
new.subject: the war

qualifiers.spatial: France, Germany, the British Empire/British Commonwealth, Balkans, Mediterranean, Middle East, East Africa, Battle, Britain

old.claim_text: After the Allied victory, Germany, Austria, Japan, and Korea were occupied, and German and Japanese leaders were put on trial for war crimes.
old.subject: the Allied victory

new.claim_text: After the Allied victory, Germany, Austria, Japan, and Korea were occupied, and German and Japanese leaders were tried for war crimes.
new.subject: the Allied victory

old.claim_text: The Soviet Union and the US emerged as rival superpowers, setting the stage for the half-century Cold War.
old.subject: The Soviet Union

new.claim_text: The Soviet Union and the US emerged as rival superpowers, setting the stage for the Cold War.
new.subject: The Soviet Union

old.claim_text: Tanks and aircraft played major roles, enabling the strategic bombing of cities and delivery of the first and only nuclear weapons ever used in war.
old.subject: Tanks

new.claim_text: Tanks and aircraft played major roles, the latter enabling the strategic bombing of cities and delivery of the only nuclear weapons used in war.
new.subject: Tanks

old.claim_text: The Soviet Red Army however halted in the Praga district on the other side of the Vistula and watched passively as the Germans quelled the Warsaw Uprising initiated by the Home Army (the main faction of the Polish resistance, loyal to the non-communist government-in exile).
old.subject: The Soviet Red Army

new.claim_text: The Soviet Red Army however halted in the Praga district on the other side of the Vistula as the Germans quelled the Warsaw Uprising initiated by the Home Army (the main faction of the Polish resistance, loyal to the non-communist government-in exile), killing over 150,000 Poles.
new.subject: The Soviet Red Army

old.claim_text: In early May 1942, Japan initiated operations to capture Port Moresby by amphibious assault and thus sever communications and supply lines between the United States and Australia.
old.subject: Japan

old.claim_text: Meanwhile, the victorious Allies of World War I, such as France, Belgium, Italy, Romania, and Greece, gained territory, and new nation-states were created out of the dissolution of the Austro-Hungarian, Ottoman, and Russian Empires (Finland, Estonia, Latvia, Lithuania, Poland, Czechoslovakia, Yugoslavia, Turkey).
old.subject: the victorious Allies

new.subject: the Axis
new.source_section: Axis attack on the Soviet Union (1941)

old.claim_text: Although the Red Army was preparing for strategic counter-offensives before the war, Operation Barbarossa forced the Soviet supreme command to adopt strategic defence.

old.claim_text: World War II is the deadliest conflict in history, causing the death of 70 to 85 million people, more than half of whom were civilians.

after the German Luftwaffe Blitz, December 30, 1940]]

old.claim_text: Most historians agree that World War II began with the German invasion of Poland on 1 September 1939
old.subject: Most historians

new.claim_text: Most historians agree that World War II began with the German invasion of Poland on 1 September 1939 and the British and French declarations of war on Germany two days later.
new.subject: Most historians

old.claim_text: Faced with an imminent Allied invasion, the prospect of further atomic bombings, and a Soviet declaration of war and invasion of Manchuria, Japan announced its unconditional surrender on 15 August, and signed a surrender document on 2 September 1945.
old.subject: Japan

new.claim_text: Japan announced its unconditional surrender on 15 August, and signed a surrender document on 2 September 1945.
new.subject: Japan

old.claim_text: Nearly all of the world's countries participated, with many nations mobilising their resources in pursuit of total war.
old.subject: Nearly all

new.claim_text: Nearly all of the world's countries participated.
new.subject: Nearly all

old.claim_text: In May, the Germans defeated Soviet offensives in the Kerch Peninsula and at Kharkov, and then in June 1942 launched their main summer offensive against southern Russia, to seize the oil fields of the Caucasus and occupy the Kuban steppe, while maintaining positions on the northern and central areas of the front.
old.subject: the Germans

new.claim_text: In May, the Germans defeated Soviet offensives in the Kerch Peninsula and at Kharkov.

added.claim_text: In 1944, the Western Allies invaded France at Normandy, and the Soviet Union advanced into Central Europe.
added.subject: the Western Allies

qualifiers.spatial: France, the Soviet Union, Central Europe

added.claim_text: During the same period, Japan suffered major setbacks, including the crippling of its navy by the United States, the loss of key Western Pacific islands, and defeats in South-Central China and Burma.
added.subject: Japan

qualifiers.spatial: Japan, the United States, Western Pacific, South-Central China, Burma

added.claim_text:
battle the Imperial Japanese Army forces in Taierzhuang, 1938]]

In July 1937, Japan captured the former Chinese imperial capital of Peking after instigating the Marco Polo Bridge incident, which culminated in the Japanese campaign to invade all of China following years of tension and low-level conflicts.
added.subject: Japan

qualifiers.spatial: Taierzhuang, Japan, Peking, the Marco Polo Bridge, China

added.claim_text: Nazi policy entailed that Wehrmacht subject Soviet POWs to murderous treatment, executing all Jewish and Communist POWs immediately per the Commissar Order, and subjecting the remainder to forced marches to open-air concentration camps, where they were to be deliberately starved to death.
added.subject: Nazi policy
added.source_section: Axis attack on the Soviet Union (1941)

added.claim_text: By the end of the winter of 1941, 2.8 million Soviet POWs had died in German captivity.
added.subject: 2.8 million Soviet POWs

qualifiers.temporal: the end of the winter of 1941

added.source_section: Axis attack on the Soviet Union (1941)

added.claim_text: Some 3.3 million Soviet POWs would die in German captivity by the war's end in total, a nearly 60% mortality rate.
added.subject: Some 3.3 million Soviet POWs
added.source_section: Axis attack on the Soviet Union (1941)

added.claim_text: The diversion of three-quarters of the Axis troops and the majority of their air forces from France and the central Mediterranean to the Eastern Front prompted the United Kingdom to reconsider its grand strategy.
added.subject: The diversion
added.source_section: Axis attack on the Soviet Union (1941)

added.claim_text: Japanese advances were accompanied by numerous atrocities, including the Sook Ching Massacre in Singapore.
added.subject: Japanese advances

added.claim_text: Following the capture of Bataan, Japanese armies forced some 75,000 Filipino and American prisoners on a 42km death march, resulting in thousands of deaths.
added.subject: Japanese armies

added.claim_text: Despite considerable losses, in early 1942 Germany and its allies stopped a major Soviet offensive in central and southern Russia, keeping most territorial gains they had achieved during the previous year.
added.subject: early 1942 Germany

added.claim_text: The fortress city of Sevastopol, which the Red Army had held out against Axis siege for nearly 250 days, was finally seized with the use of massive artillery bombardments and poison gas.
added.subject: The fortress city

added.claim_text: An estimated 60 million to more than 75 million people died in the war including at least 20 million who died from deprivation, famine and disease.
added.subject: An estimated 60 million to more than 75 million people

added.claim_text: Civilian deaths have been estimated to comprise 67% to 80% of all direct and indirect deaths from the war.
added.subject: Civilian deaths

added.claim_text: The Soviet Union had the highest overall death toll (estimated at 20 million to 28 million), [REF:
added.subject: The Soviet Union

added.claim_text:
during the Nanjing Massacre in December 1937]]
In trials following the war, representatives of the Axis powers and their accomplices were convicted of numerous war crimes, crimes against humanity and complicity in genocides.
added.subject: representatives
added.source_section: Genocide, war crimes and crimes against humanity

added.claim_text: The Nazis killed about 6 million Jews in a racially motivated genocide known as the Holocaust.
added.subject: The Nazis
added.source_section: Genocide, war crimes and crimes against humanity

added.claim_text: They also killed millions of Slavs, over 130 thousand Romani, and members of other groups that they considered racially inferior.
added.subject: They
added.source_section: Genocide, war crimes and crimes against humanity

added.claim_text: Almost 300,000 people with mental and physical disabilities were also systematically killed in Germany and its occupied territories.
added.subject: Almost 300,000 people
added.source_section: Genocide, war crimes and crimes against humanity

removed.claim_text: In June 1938, Chinese forces stalled the Japanese advance by flooding the Yellow River; this manoeuvre bought time for the Chinese to prepare their defences at Wuhan, but the city was taken by October.

Despite considerable losses, in early 1942 Germany and its allies stopped a major Soviet offensive in central and southern Russia, keeping most territorial gains they had achieved during the previous year.
removed.subject: soldiers

removed.claim_text: The Germans split Army Group South into two groups: Army Group A advanced to the lower Don River and struck south-east to the Caucasus, while Army Group B headed towards the Volga River.
removed.subject: Army Group A

removed.claim_text: The Soviets decided to make their stand at Stalingrad on the Volga.
removed.subject: The Soviets

removed.claim_text: By mid-November, the Germans had nearly taken Stalingrad in bitter street fighting.
removed.subject: the Germans

removed.claim_text: The Soviets began their second winter counter-offensive, starting with an encirclement of German forces at Stalingrad, and an assault on the Rzhev salient near Moscow, though the latter failed disastrously.
removed.subject: The Soviets

removed.claim_text: By early February 1943, the German army had taken tremendous losses; German troops at Stalingrad had been defeated, and the front-line had been pushed back beyond its position before the summer offensive.
removed.subject: German troops

removed.claim_text: In mid-February, after the Soviet push had tapered off, the Germans launched another attack on Kharkov, creating a salient in their front line around the Soviet city of Kursk.
removed.subject: February

removed.claim_text: Estimates for the total number of casualties in the war vary, because many deaths went unrecorded.
